data.text: I’ve confirmed the current Axon backend already has a GraphQL branch in `ReadAll`/`WriteAll`, so the question is whether the CRUD methods are still routed through local snapshots or whether this bead is already partially implemented. I’m reading the lower half of the backend and the existing Axon tests now to determine the exact gap before editing.

I’ve confirmed the store already composes CRUD/claim/dep/event behavior on top of `RawBackend`, so the likely remaining work is inside the Axon transport path and its persistence semantics, not the high-level API. I’m running the focused Axon tests now to see which acceptance items are actually failing and whether the GraphQL branch is incomplete or just subtly wrong.

The five targeted tests already pass in the current tree, which means the bead is probably about tightening the implementation contract rather than adding brand-new behavior. I’m checking for any remaining Axon code paths that still treat the filesystem snapshots as authoritative when a GraphQL client is present, because that is the remaining acceptance item that can still be violated despite green tests.
